title: Zosterophyllanthos allenii
intro: Zosterophyllanthos allenii, also known as the Allen's Zostera, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae, native to Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in Western Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues, likely due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Zosterophyllanthos allenii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Zosterophyllanthos allenii is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
